writingStyleRules: Use imperatives for CTAs (e.g., 'Shop the Sale', not 'You can shop the sale')
Never start a sentence with 'We are excited to...'
Keep subject lines under 50 characters where possible
Lead with the punchline, not the setup
emailGuidelines: Subject lines: deadpan or absurdist, never salesy. Preheader: extends the joke or adds urgency without being pushy. CTAs: short imperatives, 2-4 words. Body: product-forward, let the product description carry the humor — don't over-explain the joke. Avoid countdown timers and fake scarcity.
legalConstraints: All emails must include a functioning unsubscribe link. Subject lines must not be deceptive or misleading (CAN-SPAM). Discount claims must be accurate and verifiable. Do not use 'free' in subject lines without an offer that is genuinely free.

title: Flash Sale
copyTone: Immediate urgency — hours, not days. FOMO-driven. Short sentences. Every word earns its place.

title: New Launch
copyTone: Excitement and novelty. First-mover energy. Make the reader feel like they're getting early access. Avoid overselling.

title: Evergreen
copyTone: Low-pressure. Informational and helpful. Focus on product utility and personality rather than urgency.

title: The Rubber Duck Pro Max
description: Enterprise-grade debugging companion. 3x the size of a standard duck. Maintains eye contact while you explain your code out loud to an inanimate object. Now with judgment-free quacking.

title: Noise-Canceling Hoodie
description: Won't actually cancel noise, but the hood-up universal signal reduces meeting invitations by 60%. Comes pre-loaded with headphone-shaped lumps for added deterrence.

title: LGTM Keycap Set
description: Every key says LGTM. Approve pull requests at mass-production speed. Side effects may include mass-approving your coworker's 2,000-line PR without reading it.

title: The 404 Socks (3-Pack)
description: Guaranteed to go missing. If you find a matching pair, clear your cache and try again. Made from 100% organic cotton that was briefly available at this URL.

title: Merge Conflict Resolution Candle
description: Scented with lavender, cedar, and the faint aroma of regret. Light this candle when git pull ruins your afternoon. Burns for 40 hours or one rebase, whichever comes first.

title: The Standup Survival Thermos
description: Insulated for 12 hours. Outlasts any meeting that should have been a Slack message. Engraved with "No blockers" on the side so you don't even have to speak.

name: High Spenders — Last 90 Days
description: Subscribers who placed an order of $150+ in the last 90 days.
affinityDescription: High-value repeat buyers. Likely to purchase again with minimal persuasion. Respond well to exclusivity and early-access framing.

name: Lapsed — No Open 90+ Days
description: Subscribers who have not opened an email in 90 or more days.
affinityDescription: Disengaged subscribers who need a reason to re-engage. Humor and self-awareness tend to outperform typical urgency tactics with this group.

name: New Subscribers — Last 30 Days
description: Subscribers who joined in the last 30 days.
affinityDescription: First-time audience who opted in recently. Still forming their opinion of the brand. Welcome them into the voice before going hard on sales.

title: Deploy Friday Sale
primaryMessage: Deploy Friday is back. 30% off everything, no exclusions. You know the rule — don't deploy on Fridays. But nobody said anything about shopping.
supportingMessage: Same products. Same quality. 30% cheaper than Monday. The only difference is you shouldn't be at your computer right now.
valueProposition: 30% off everything sitewide. No minimum order. Free shipping on orders $50+.

tokens.description: Klaviyo-generated discount code for this send

subjectLine: It's Friday. Don't deploy. Do shop. 30% off.
preheader: Your CI pipeline can wait. This sale can't.
